text: Ever wonder why you see lightning before you hear thunder? Light travels at about 299,792 kilometers per second. That’s way faster than sound, which is just 343 meters per second! So when lightning strikes, the light reaches you instantly. But sound? It takes its sweet time. For every five seconds between the flash and the boom, you're roughly one mile away from the strike. So next time you see lightning, count the seconds until you hear thunder. That’s your distance check! Like what you learned? Hit that follow button for more mind-blowing science facts!
